---
title: "Your first agent session"
---

&nbsp;

::: {.feature-card}
**tl;dr** --- Get crosslink running and watch your agent handle a task with persistent memory --- in under a minute.
:::

&nbsp;

<!-- ![](../assets/img/quickstart.gif) -->

## 1. Install and initialize

```bash
cargo install crosslink
cd your-project
crosslink init
```

This creates `.crosslink/` (config, rules, database), `.claude/hooks/` (behavioral hooks), and `.claude/settings.json` (hook wiring). See [Installation](installation.qmd) for more install options.

&nbsp;

---

## 2. Start a session

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="35%"}
**You say:**

> "Let's get started"

Every conversation begins with a session. If a previous session left handoff notes, your agent sees them immediately.
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink session start
```

On the first session this is a no-op. On subsequent sessions, handoff notes from the previous agent (or your past self) appear automatically.
:::
:::

&nbsp;

---

## 3. Create an issue

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="35%"}
**You say:**

> "Fix the login bug — it's high priority"
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink quick "Fix login bug" \
  -p high -l bug
```

One command: creates the issue, labels it, and marks it as the active work item.
:::
:::

&nbsp;

---

## 4. Work with breadcrumbs

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="35%"}
**You say:**

> "Dig into the auth module and find the root cause"

Your agent explores the code, leaving breadcrumbs as it goes. These survive context compression — if the window fills up, the trail of what was found and decided persists.
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
# Breadcrumb — survives context compression
crosslink session action \
  "Found root cause in refresh_token()"

# Permanent comment on the issue
crosslink issue comment 1 \
  "Token wasn't refreshed on 401"

# ... implements the fix, runs tests ...
```
:::
:::

&nbsp;

---

## 5. Commit and close

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="35%"}
**You say:**

> "Commit that and close the issue"

The agent uses the built-in `/commit` skill, which creates the commit and records a result comment on the active issue. Closing auto-appends a changelog entry.
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
# /commit stages, writes the message,
# and records the result on the issue
/commit

crosslink close 1
```
:::
:::

&nbsp;

---

## 6. End the session

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="35%"}
**You say:**

> "Wrap up and leave notes for next time"
:::
::: {.column width="5%"}
→
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink session end \
  --notes "Fixed auth bug #1. Dark mode is next."
```

The next session — whether it is you, another agent, or the same agent after a restart — sees these handoff notes automatically.
:::
:::

&nbsp;

---

## What's next?

- [Session Workflow](../guides/session-workflow.qmd) — deep dive into session management
- [Design Workflow](../guides/design-workflow.qmd) — iterate on design documents before implementation
- [Kickoff](../guides/kickoff.qmd) — launch autonomous background agents
- [Swarm Orchestration](../guides/swarm.qmd) — multi-agent phased builds
- [Knowledge Management](../guides/knowledge.qmd) — shared research across agents
- [Web Dashboard](../guides/web-dashboard.qmd) — browser-based project oversight
- [Terminal Dashboard](../guides/tui.qmd) — interactive TUI for browsing issues
- [Maintenance](../guides/maintenance.qmd) — pruning and health checks
- [Multi-Agent](../guides/multi-agent.qmd) — coordinate multiple AI agents
- [Hooks Guide](../guides/hooks.qmd) — behavioral guardrails for AI coding
- [CLI Reference](../reference/commands.qmd) — full command reference
